tracks.meaning: a tube or hollow cylinder made of metal, plastic, or other material used to convey liquids, gases, or other substances
a tubular wind instrument played by blowing air through it to produce sound
to convey or transport something through a pipe or pipeline system
any tubular vessel or duct in the body, particularly those carrying fluids
a channel or means of transmitting information or data
a mechanism for passing data or output from one process or program to another sequentially
a smoking device with a bowl and stem for holding and smoking tobacco or other substances
a cylindrical conduit or channel in machinery or equipment for directing flow or movement
a boatswain's whistle used to signal commands aboard a ship
to store or hold something for later use, especially in computing or data contexts
